description: Skill must invoke render-status.sh --if-stale so the cache regens lazily (not on every call)
Skill must document the cache file path ~/.cache/brain-os/status.md so the reader knows what to Read
Skill must document the dirty marker path so the hook + script share one contract
Skill must describe the -f flag behavior (force regen by omitting --if-stale)
Skill must have a ## Config block pointing at brain-os.config.md so vault_path + gh_task_repo can be resolved
Skill must document the STATUS_CACHE_MAX_AGE safety net — external gh mutations bypass the hook, so a max-age backstop is required

Skill must document the SessionStart invalidation — session-start-tasks.sh touches status.dirty so the first /status per session always reflects between-session GH mutations the dirty-marker hook missed (eventual consistency / stale-regen race).
Email Focus must filter by user_category from the dual-perspective taxonomy (ai-brain#100 Phase 2) — replaces the old me-personal/me-business owner-bucket filter
Spec must name the two categories that surface inline (r-user, team-sla-at-risk) so implementers know what to filter on

Output format must document the 🔄 stealth-picked marker + pickup footnote — Ready issues that look picked up in another session without status:in-progress flip, detected via recent vault commit referencing #N OR recent handover/grill/task file matching the issue number.

Skill prose must not contain the literal `gh issue close` instruction — the central close helper (close-issue.sh, wired in #160) is the single close site, and any direct gh issue close mention re-introduces the ghost-label leak surface. The max-age-safety-net narrative may still describe external GH mutations from a plain terminal, but worded so the literal `gh issue close` token does not appear.

Spec must document the parent→child tree contract for the Stories sub-block — covers AC#1 (tree shape, not flat list) and signals to readers that the block is rendered by build-stories-tree.ts, not the legacy flat formatter. (#262)
Spec must enumerate the status markers (READY, in-prog, BLOCKED, backlog, closed) so AC#2 (status markers per node match status:* label at render time) has a single source of truth. (#262)

Spec must explain the closed-sibling collapse rule + that an open sibling between closed siblings breaks the run (AC#3 — `#A–#B, #C [closed] — N done` formatting, with run-splitting on open interleave). (#262)

Spec must describe the within-story `(blocked by #N)` suffix derived from `## Blocked by` parsing — closed blockers + non-sibling blockers are silenced. Surfaced from the user-added requirement to show within-story dependencies. (#262)

Spec must describe the cross-story phase pairing — title regex `— Phase N` extracts project + phase, and an active root with a backlog Phase-N+1 peer gets a trailing `Next story queued: #M` leaf. Surfaced from the user's `multi-phase project` follow-up. (#262)
Spec must document the global `Next story queued: #N — title` footer line — highest-priority unlinked bench plan (AC#4). Hidden when no qualifying bench plan exists. (#262)

Spec must declare that the tree-build logic lives in build-stories-tree.ts with bun-test fixtures (NOT bash), per the global TS+bun-test rule. Enforces the no-fix-bug-via-bash-wrapper invariant in the Rules section. (#262)

Spec must document the anti-drift stale-story contract — active stories whose subtree has no GitHub activity for > STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS days are surfaced in the ⚠️ Stale stories section above all normal sections. (ai-brain#178)
Spec must name STALE_THRESHOLD_DAYS as the staleness threshold and state it is a constant (KISS), not an env var — so the threshold source is unambiguous. (ai-brain#178)

Spec must declare the staleness signal is max updatedAt over the subtree (repo-agnostic), NOT the issue's original per-file git-log sketch — so the design rationale survives future edits. (ai-brain#178)
